pr_title: Change is_visible to check if the center of an element is visible since this the part Selenium's Element.click will attempt to click on
pr_body:
Currently click(...) checks if any part of an element is visible before attempting to scroll using scroll_into_view. Selenium's Element.click will also attempt to scroll the element into the view port before clicking. Unlike scroll_into_view, however, Selenium's scrolling will scroll the element to the top of the view port, resulting in problems with display: fixed nav elements overlaying the element to be clicked.

Selenium's Element.click attempts to click the center of an element. To make sure our scrolling gets used rather than Selenium's scrolling, we must change the visibility test to check if the centre of the element is off-viewport rather than checking if any part of the element within the viewport.

"Recognize" method is a bidi-streaming method, which is not
supported by Gapic codegen, but yet it should exist when
we want to support it for some language.

Anyways streaming methods will be ignored as
https://github.com/googleapis/toolkit/pull/328

pr_title: moved files from lower to upper case Keysight folder
pr_body:
# Problem: separate 'Keysight' and 'keysight' folders in qcodes/instrument_drivers

From @jenshnielsen I learned that on Windows and MacOsX the path queries to the file system are case insensitive, while on Linux they are not. You can see the two folder in the repository as it is presented by the github page.
So on a Windows machine there is only one visible Keysight folder (the capitalized one), that contains the files of both folders.
A possible scenario in which this could have happened, is that a user created locally a folder on a windows machine, and subsequently merged with a new version from main repository, in which the same  folder has been added but with a different case choice.

### Damage
This pull request simply moves files from the lower case keysight folder to the upper case one. This choice minimized the damage by only breaking the code that uses the affected drivers for Linux systems. 

### New Standard?
I propose to discuss the possibility of a standard for the use of capital letters for all new folders in the Qcodes repository.

pr_title: Add explicit tracking of hosts for pssh greenlets
pr_body:
Specific changes, for `ParallelSSHClient`:
- `run_command`, `copy_file`, and `copy_remote_file` now each return a
  list of (host, greenlet) tuples instead of a list of greenlets.
- The `cmds` attribute now stores a list of (host, greenlet) tuples
  instead of a list of greenlets.
- `get_output` now requires a `host` argument, because the above changes
  mean the host is always known, and having access to it enables the
  function to *always* correctly update the host output structure, even
  in cases where the `cmd` greenlet threw an exception with no `host`
  argument.
- Modified tests to work with the above changes.

Overall, this results in a simplification of usage where parallel
command/copy exceptions are being explicitly, individually handled by
the calling code, and only a very slight increase in complexity for
usages where such exceptions are being ignored, or are being managed for
an entire group (e.g. via `joinall(..., raise_error=True)` or
`run_command(..., stop_on_errors=True)`).


## Motivation
My own usage for this right now is in capturing and handling SFTP failures per-host, e.g. something along the lines of:
```python
for host, greenlet in client.copy_file(some_file, a_file_name):
    try:
        greenlet.get()
    except Exception as ex:
        logging.error("SFTP failure for '{}', caught exception:\n{}".format(
            host, ex))
        handle_sftp_exception(host, ex)
```

One day, when current high priority issues are done, we will parse route files (routing.py's, url.py's etc.) to find controllers so we can run it at scale with less false positives then we can use the apaptor pattern.

But until then, requiring users to point pyt at their controllers seems fine. We can do this with the template pattern instead, for now. In Pylons for instance, in a controller file anything without a leading underscore should have arguments marked as tainted. This and the flask helper function works great.

The Flask option is still the default, so the default behavior hasn't changed. I was running pyt on DjangoNV (a purposely vulnerable Django app) and it was missing a lot because I couldn't mark all args as tainted, this PR solves that.

python -m unittest riak.tests.test_all.RiakHttpPoolTransportTestCase
did not check the content of the links, only that the right number
of links are returned so the "foo3" bucket with a link containing
special characters "tag2!@#%^&*)" results in the following when
retrieved "tag2%21%40%23%25%5E%26%2A%29"

Fixes Issue #75
